description:
Diabetic & BP patients in India might find it difficult to find an affordable health insurance plan with less waiting periods as both these disease have a waiting period of around 2-3 years.

In this video, we have shared 4 health insurance plans with BP & Diabetes cover from Day 1 and compared them on the basis of features, inclusions , waiting periods as well as the premiums.

Top 4 Health Insurance Plans discussed :-
1. Niva Bupa Aspire : https://youtu.be/f0_m_6fBdrM?si=_gIdg8sqCXOnpEtX
2. Aditya Birla Activ One - MAX : https://youtu.be/4LTtX0is2GE?si=UIndtIj6LpZW6g_I
3. ICICI Lombard Elevate Plan : https://youtu.be/cM0qA-GJwFE?si=6WEMZfI7Y9nrj5Kg
4. HDFC ERGO ENERGY PLAN : https://youtu.be/W9MKhaaGDyo?si=BzkVdAU_9_WWtSGP

WATCH THE VIDEO FOR MORE DETAILS : -

I am writing to express my concern regarding the rejection of my claim for my mother's recent hospitalization. Despite confirming with insurance team prior to admission due to her emergency medical condition, and submitting all required documents on March 2nd, I received notification after 30 days that my claim has been rejected.

I would greatly appreciate it if you could provide a detailed explanation for the rejection, as all documents submitted were genuine and in order. Please assist me in understanding the reasoning behind this decision and guide me on the next steps to take. Please help for claim receive.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Niva Bupa Policy 34443606202400 ( Claim ID - 2166749 )

comments.text: Is there any waiting period if we shift from Reassure to reassure 2.0 if we show BP after no claim for four year with Niva Bupa and renewal is in November
comments.author: @dhirengala1443

comments.text: No. It isn't any pre condition. It happened after taking policy so everything will be covered. But please do confirm with them
comments.author: @v_a_n_AR

comments.text:
Yes, no waiting period if you are keeping the same sum assured.

In case, you are increasing the Sum insured, waiting period will apply on the increased sum assured
comments.author: @GurleenKaurTikku

comments.text:
Without insulin.
Only with tablets. 
Diabetes has to be under control only. They offer the policy to people with hba1c of under 7 only.

comments.text:
Both actually - Type 1 and Type 2

Companies accept policies as per their underwriting guidelines which keep on changing from time to time
